title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.

title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
summary: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.

title: Modi Courts Indo-Pacific Partners as China, US Reshape Region
summary: From missiles and uranium to critical minerals and sports diplomacy, Prime Minister Narendra Modi used a three-nation tour to deepen India’s Indo-Pacific partnerships as regional powers strengthen ties amid China’s growing influence and uncertainty over US engagement.

title: Warsh and US Inflation Will Set Tone for July Fed Decision
summary: Kevin Warsh is about to make his first appearance before Congress as Federal Reserve chairman, and during two days of testimony he’ll have new US inflation data to parse with lawmakers.

title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.

title: What Is Robinhood Chain? The Ethereum Layer-2 Network for Tokenized Stocks
summary: Robinhood Chain is an Ethereum layer-2 network built with Arbitrum technology for tokenized assets, crypto apps, and on-chain financial products.

title: Nano Banana 2 Lite vs. Nano Banana 2: When to Save Your Money and When to Upgrade

title: Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend hit for $9 million after Supra verifier accepts manipulated price update

title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
summary: The preceding eight weeks drained a combined $9.46 billion from the two groups, meaning this week’s rebound recovered only about 3% of those outflows.

title: How AI rebrands fail to deliver a lasting share price boost

title: What history tells us about Trump’s self-enrichment
summary: The blurring of public and private interests in the modern White House has many precedents — but it may yet test the tolerance of American voters
